lesson.menu_title: My Story -- Past Simple for Professional and Personal Narrative
lesson.menu_desc: Tell your whole career, from nineteen to today -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 3 -- My Story -- Past Simple for Professional and Personal Narrative
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 3 -- Past Simple for Your Story

listenings.text: Good evening, everyone. Tonight, I am happy to introduce a special guest from Brazil. Maria Clara started her career very young, at nineteen years old. She worked in public service for many years. She studied a lot, and she finished two doctorate degrees. She led a team of eight people, and she became a leader in education. Last year, she traveled to China and to Seoul. Her story is a story of hard work. Please welcome Maria Clara.

Hello, Maria Clara. This is Grace from the office. I called about the meeting yesterday. Thank you for your presentation. You spoke very well, and the guests liked your story. The director asked for your slides, so I sent them this morning. We also planned the next round table for Friday. It was a great day. Have a good evening, and see you soon.

extra_audio.text: Hello. My name is Maria Clara. I started my career at nineteen years old. I studied at two universities, and I led a team for many years. Today, I represent my institution around the world.

items.situation: A journalist asks: "Can you tell me about your background?"
tips: I started my career at nineteen, in public service.
Start with when + what you did
I studied at USP and UFSC.
I led a team of eight people.
It was a big challenge, but I used to study every night.
I used to + base verb, for a past habit
The day my granddaughter said my name in English.
was, for one moment in the past

items.situation: The journalist speaks fast and you miss a word.
tips: Sorry, I didn't catch that. Could you repeat, please?
Asking to repeat is control, not weakness.
